# pfsense_firewall_nat_outbound (Resource)

[Firewall NAT Outbound](https://docs.netgate.com/pfsense/en/latest/nat/outbound.html). Outbound NAT rules control how traffic leaving the firewall is translated.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Outbound NAT rule using interface address
resource "pfsense_firewall_nat_outbound" "lan_to_wan" {
  interface           = "wan"
  protocol            = "tcp"
  source_address      = "192.168.1.0/24"
  destination_address = "any"
  target              = "(self)"
  description         = "LAN to WAN outbound NAT"
}

# Outbound NAT rule with specific translation IP
resource "pfsense_firewall_nat_outbound" "dmz_to_wan" {
  interface           = "wan"
  source_address      = "10.0.2.0/24"
  destination_address = "any"
  target              = "other-subnet"
  target_ip           = "203.0.113.10"
  target_ip_subnet    = "32"
  pool_options        = "round-robin"
  description         = "DMZ outbound NAT with specific IP"
}

# NAT exclusion rule (no NAT)
resource "pfsense_firewall_nat_outbound" "vpn_no_nat" {
  interface           = "wan"
  source_address      = "192.168.1.0/24"
  destination_address = "10.10.0.0/16"
  target              = ""
  no_nat              = true
  description         = "No NAT for VPN traffic"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `description` (String) Description used as the unique identifier for this NAT outbound rule.
- `interface` (String) Network interface this outbound NAT rule applies to (e.g. 'wan', 'lan', 'opt1').

### Optional

- `destination_address` (String) Destination network. Can be 'any', a CIDR network, an alias, or a special pfSense interface address. Defaults to 'any'.
- `destination_not` (Boolean) Invert the destination address match.
- `destination_port` (String) Destination port or port range. Only applicable for TCP, UDP, or TCP/UDP protocols.
- `disabled` (Boolean) Disable this outbound NAT rule.
- `nat_port` (String) Translation port. If empty, the original port is preserved.
- `no_nat` (Boolean) Disable NAT for matching traffic (exclusion rule).
- `no_sync` (Boolean) Do not synchronize this rule to HA peer.
- `pool_options` (String) Pool options for translation address. Options: `''`, `'round-robin'`, `'round-robin sticky-address'`, `'random'`, `'random sticky-address'`, `'source-hash'`, `'bitmask'`.
- `protocol` (String) Protocol. Options: `''`, `'tcp'`, `'udp'`, `'tcp/udp'`, `'icmp'`, `'esp'`, `'ah'`, `'gre'`, `'ipv6'`, `'igmp'`, `'pim'`, `'ospf'`. Empty string matches any protocol.
- `source_address` (String) Source network. Can be 'any', a CIDR network (e.g. '192.168.1.0/24'), an alias, or a special pfSense interface address. Defaults to 'any'.
- `source_hash_key` (String) Source hash key (hex string) used when pool_options is 'source-hash'.
- `source_not` (Boolean) Invert the source address match.
- `source_port` (String) Source port or port range. Only applicable for TCP, UDP, or TCP/UDP protocols.
- `static_nat_port` (Boolean) Do not randomize the source port for outgoing connections (static port).
- `target` (String) Translation address type. Empty string means interface address, '(self)' for self, 'other-subnet' for other subnet, or a specific IP address.
- `target_ip` (String) Translation IP address when target is a specific IP or 'other-subnet'.
- `target_ip_subnet` (String) Translation subnet bits when target is 'other-subnet' (e.g. '24').

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Rule index (position) in the outbound NAT rule list.

## Import

Import is supported using the rule index (position):

```shell
terraform import pfsense_firewall_nat_outbound.example 0
```
